title: Thalictrum foetidum lusus glaucum
intro: Thalictrum foetidum lusus glaucum, also known as Foetid Meadow-rue or Glaucous Meadow-rue, is a subspecies of the Thalictrum plant, a flowering plant native to the Americas and Asia, characterized by its distinctive, fern-like foliage and clusters of small, nodding flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of inflammation, fever reduction, and antimicrobial effects due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Thalictrum foetidum lusus glaucum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory infections.

However, I can tell you that Thalictrum is a genus of flowering plants in the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. The genus Thalictrum includes approximately 70 species, most of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. They are typically found in woodland edges, rocky slopes, and other moist areas. Thalictrum foetidum lusus glaucum is a variant of the species Thalictrum foetidum.
